[Moderate] Special Weather Statement: Special Weather Statement issued May 29 at 3:22AM CDT by NWS Duluth MN — Dry conditions with relative humidity values between 20 and 25 percent are forecast this afternoon and early evening for portions of northeast Minnesota north of a line from near Pine River to Grand Rapids to Orr. These dry conditions may cause fires to spread. Check burning restrictions and fire danger before burning. For more information on burning restrictions for Minnesota, see ww

noaa-aggregate

[Moderate] Special Weather Statement: Special Weather Statement issued May 29 at 5:25AM EDT by NWS Gaylord MI — The combination of drying fuels, low relative humidity values, and gusty winds will result in elevated fire danger conditions across northern lower Michigan today. West to northwest winds with sustained speeds between 7-12 mph will gust to 20-30 mph at times into this evening. Relative humidity values between 20-30 percent are expected across parts of northern Michigan from late mornin

[Moderate] Special Weather Statement: Special Weather Statement issued May 29 at 5:45AM EDT by NWS Charleston SC — A cold front is expected to push south over the South Carolina Lowcountry this morning. As the front approaches, fog should g

noaa-aggregate

[Moderate] Special Weather Statement: Special Weather Statement issued May 29 at 5:45AM EDT by NWS Charleston SC — A cold front is expected to push south over the South Carolina Lowcountry this morning. As the front approaches, fog should gradually develop across portions of the Lowcountry and southeast Georgia early this morning. The fog may become locally dense, reducing visibilities on area roadways. The fog should generally dissipate by 730 AM.

[NWS FFC] Area Forecast Discussion 2026-05-29T07:26 — 000 FXUS62 KFFC 290726 AFDFFC Area Forecast Discussion National Weather Service Peachtree City GA 326 AM EDT Fri May 29 2026 ...Morning Area Forecast Discussion... .KEY MESSAGES... - Scattered to numerous showers and thunderstorms can be expected across the area through the beginning of next week. - Locally heavy rainfall will remain the primary concern in any thunderstorms given the continued moist environment in place.
